'''Liam Watts''' (born 8 July 1990) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He is an [[England Knights]] international.

He has previously played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan at [[Gateshead Thunder (2000)|Gateshead Thunder]] and [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Watts was born in [[Featherstone]],<ref name="YP25Mar19"/> [[West Yorkshire]], England, and he was a junior at [[Featherstone Lions]].

==Career==
=== Castleford Tigers ===
Watts made his first team debut and scored a try for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in an 88-10 victory over [[Castleford Lock Lane]] in the [[2007 Challenge Cup#Round 3|Challenge Cup third round]] on 11 March 2007.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2007-03-11|title=Castleford vs Lock Lane|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/castleford-vs-lock-lane/15370|access-date=2022-01-03|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
He made his [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] debut in a Challenge Cup fourth round tie in April 2008,<ref name="BBC18Apr08">{{cite web |title=Morgan turns to youth for Cup tie |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/7353253.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=4 July 2019 |date=18 April 2008}}</ref> and signed a two-year contract until the end of the 2010 season in June 2008.<ref name="BBC3Jun08">{{cite web |title=Watts handed new Hull KR contract |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull_kr/7432564.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=4 July 2019 |date=3 June 2008}}</ref> 
[[File:Liam Watts.JPG|thumb|right|140px|Watts playing for Hull KR in 2010]]
After a fine season in 2010 in which Watts played 26 of 27 [[Super League]] matches, he was called up to the England training squad for the Four Nations Championship in September 2010,<ref name="BBC20Sep10">{{cite web |title=Liam Watts and Scott Murrell in shock England call-up |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9016584.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=4 July 2019 |date=20 September 2010}}</ref> and awarded the 2010 Albert Goldthorpe Rookie of the Year Medal.<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|accessdate=16 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140326074057/http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|archive-date=26 March 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref>

====Gateshead Thunder (loan)====
In 2009 he spent time on loan from Hull KR at [[Gateshead Thunder (2000)|Gateshead Thunder]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

===Hull FC===
Watts requested to be released from his contract with Hull Kingston Rovers in June 2012,<ref name="BBC11Jun12">{{cite web |title=Prop Liam Watts requests Hull Kingston Rovers release |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18394248 |website=BBC Sport |accessdate=4 July 2019 |date=11 June 2012}}</ref> and joined [[Hull F.C.]] on a three-and-a-half-year contract.<ref name="BBC26Sep18">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18555486|title=Hull FC sign prop Liam Watts from rivals Hull KR|work=BBC Sport|date=22 June 2012|access-date=26 September 2018}}</ref> Hull coach [[Peter Gentle]] said: "Liam is certainly a promising player for the future and we are looking forward to helping him realise his potential".<ref name="SS22Jun14">{{cite web |title=Hull sign up Watts |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/7835603/hull-sign-up-watts |website=Sky Sports |accessdate=4 July 2019 |date=22 June 2014}}</ref>  He signed a new three-year contract in July 2014,<ref name="SS30Jul14">{{cite web |title=Super League: Liam Watts signs new three-year deal with Hull FC |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9399713/super-league-liam-watts-signs-new-three-year-deal-with-hull-fc |website=Sky Sports |accessdate=4 July 2019 |date=30 July 2014}}</ref> and a further two-year contract in March 2017.<ref name="SS17Mar17">{{cite web |title=Liam Watts and Jordan Abdull extend stay at Hull FC |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/10805414/liam-watts-and-jordan-abdull-extend-stay-at-hull-fc |website=Sky Sports |accessdate=4 July 2019 |date=17 March 2017}}</ref>

He won the [[Challenge Cup]] with Hull in 2016<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref> and 2017 at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="BBC14Mar18">{{cite news|date=14 March 2018|title=Liam Watts: Castleford Tigers sign Hull FC prop for 'significant fee'|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43402864|accessdate=26 September 2018}}</ref>

====Doncaster (loan)====
He spent time on loan from [[Hull F.C.|Hull]], playing for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in 2014.

===Castleford Tigers===
Watts signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] on a three-year deal in March 2018.<ref name="BBC14Mar18" /> Hull had agreed a fee with [[RFL Championship|Championship]] side [[Toronto Wolfpack]], but Castleford's "last-minute significant offer" saw him return to [[The Jungle (Wheldon Road)|Wheldon Road]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2018-03-14|title=Liam Watts joins Castleford Tigers from Hull FC|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/11289495/liam-watts-joins-castleford-tigers-from-hull-fc|access-date=2022-01-03|website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He made 23 appearances in his first year.

Watts had an outstanding personal season in 2019, playing in all but 2 of Castleford's games and earning a place in the [[Super League Dream Team#2019|Super League Dream Team]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-09-15|title=Super League Dream Team: St Helens quintet included in 2019 line-up|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49707472|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> At the club's end of season awards, he was named Player of the Year and Players' Player of the Year, and won the inaugural Immortals Award voted for by Castleford legends.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-09-15|title=Watts At The Treble On Awards Night|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6587|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200605212152/https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6587|archive-date=2020-06-05|access-date=2022-01-03|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He was also named as the Castleford Tigers Supporters Club player of the year.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2019-09-15|title=2019 CTSC PLAYER OF THE YEAR AWARDS NIGHT, Liam Watts and Kelsey Gentles win top awards. Report and photos.|url=http://www.ctsc2.org.uk/index.php/news/2019-ctsc-player-year-awards-night-liam-watts-and-kelsey-gentles-win-top-awards-report-and-photos|access-date=2022-01-03|website=www.ctsc2.org.uk}}</ref>

In July 2019, he signed a three-year extension until the end of the 2023 season.<ref name="BBC4Jul19">{{cite web|date=4 July 2019|title=Liam Watts: Castleford Tigers prop agrees three-year contract extension|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/48867494|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=4 July 2019}}</ref>

On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

Watts played 20 games for Castleford in the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]] as the club finished 11th on the table and narrowly avoided relegation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/|title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed'|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=22 September 2023 }}</ref>

In the opening match of the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]], Watts was controversially sent off for a high tackle on [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s [[Tyler Dupree]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=David |date=17 February 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Craig Lingard slams decisions after Liam Watts red card sees Wigan Warriors ease home |url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/liam-watts-castleford-wigan-warriors-32151154 |access-date=18 February 2024 |website=The Mirror}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Pattison |first=Cam |date=17 February 2024 |title=Why Castleford Tigers star was controversially red carded |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/why-castleford-tigers-star-was-controversially-red-carded-88215/ |access-date=18 February 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> under new laws introduced to Super League.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=17 February 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778 |access-date=18 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=26 March 2024 |title='Made an example of': Castleford Tigers prop Liam Watts questions consistency as crackdown eases |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/made-an-example-of-castleford-tigers-prop-liam-watts-questions-consistency-as-crackdown-eases-4568445 |access-date=9 August 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

On 27 February 2025, Castleford announced that Watts had left the club by mutual agreement.<ref>{{Cite web |date=27 February 2025 |title=Veteran forward Watts leaves Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c17qqz7e0xko |access-date=28 February 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=27 February 2025 |title=Liam Watts leaves the Tigers |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=9155 |access-date=27 February 2025 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
On 3 March 2025 it was reported that he had re-joined [[Hull FC]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=3 Mar 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Liam Watts’ new club confirmed following Castleford Tigers exit|url=https://www.totalrl.com/liam-watts-new-club-confirmed-following-castleford-tigers-exit/}}</ref>

On 23 September 2025 it was announced that he had left [[Hull FC]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=23 Sep 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Liam Watts’ exit from Hull FC confirmed|url=https://www.totalrl.com/liam-watts-exit-from-hull-fc-confirmed/}}</ref>

===Goole Vikings===
On 5 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Jan 2026|accessdate=6 Jan 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Liam Watts lands Championship deal for 2026 following Hull FC release|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/liam-watts-joins-goole-vikings-for-2026}}</ref>

==International career==
===England Knights===
Watts played for the [[England Knights]] in 2011.{{cn|date=March 2023}}

===England===
Good performances for Castleford saw Watts called up for the England Performance squad in March 2019.<ref name="HL26Mar19">{{cite web |last1=Jackson |first1=William |title=Former Hull FC prop Liam Watts 'shocked' by England call-up |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/former-hull-fc-prop-liam-2687833 |website=Hull Live |accessdate=4 July 2019 |date=26 March 2019}}</ref><ref name="YP25Mar19">{{cite web |last1=Craven |first1=Dave |title=Castleford Tigers' Liam Watts left surprised as he gets call-up for England |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-tigers-liam-watts-left-surprised-as-he-gets-call-up-for-england-1-9671934 |website=Yorkshire Post |accessdate=4 July 2019 |date=25 March 2019}}</ref>

===England 9s===
He was selected in [[England 9s]] squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref name="Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-tomkins-eager-lift-inaugural-20595184|title=Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under|publisher=Mirror|date=16 October 2019|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref>
